title: stylidium beaugleholei
intro: Stylidium beaugleholei, also known as the Beauglehole's trigger plant, is a rare and endemic species of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine, and research has shown that it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Stylidium beaugleholei include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to combat infections. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which are applied topically to aid in healing and to treat various health conditions.
